turns.content: ok so my mom's birthday is in 2 weeks and I want to do something really special for her but I am the WORST at planning things help me
How fun! What kind of things does your mom enjoy? And what's your budget looking like — are we talking a big party or something more intimate?

she's turning 55 which feels like a big one to me. she's not a huge party person honestly she'd be happy with just family together but I want to do more than just showing up with a cake from Jewel you know?? budget is like... $200 max because I'm trying to be responsible with money right now lol

Okay, $200 and a woman who values family over flash — we can absolutely work with that. A few ideas: you could organize a surprise family dinner at her favorite restaurant, or do a homemade dinner at her place where everyone brings a dish. Or something more personal like a photo book or a video montage from family members. What do you think she'd react to the most?

oh my god a video montage would make her SOB she is so emotional lol. like she cried at a commercial for laundry detergent last week because it was about a mom doing her kid's laundry before they leave for college. so yeah a video would destroy her in the best way

Ha, that's adorable! Okay so a video montage could be really special and it's basically free — you'd just need to collect clips from family members. You could ask everyone to record a short message saying what they love about her or a favorite memory, and then edit them together. There are free apps like CapCut or iMovie that make it pretty easy. How many family members would you want to include?

ok so there's me, my brother Carlos, my tia Rosa and tia Lupe, my abuela if we can figure out how to get her to record on her phone lol she still has trouble with FaceTime, and then some of her friends from church. maybe like 10-12 people total??

That's a perfect number — enough for a meaningful video without it being overwhelming to organize. For your abuela, could you or Carlos visit her and record her clip in person? That way she doesn't have to figure out the tech, and her clip will probably be the most emotional one anyway. For everyone else, send a group text with clear instructions: record a 30-60 second video in horizontal mode, say one thing you love about [mom's name] or share a favorite memory, and send it to you by a specific deadline.

horizontal mode is KEY because I know my tia Lupe is going to send me a vertical video with her thumb over the camera otherwise lmao. ok and then for the actual day should I do dinner too?? or is the video enough

The video is the emotional centerpiece, but pairing it with a dinner makes it an event. You could do a potluck-style dinner at your place or your mom's — everyone brings a dish, so it's cheap and it means she doesn't have to cook on her birthday. Then after dinner you surprise her with the video on a laptop or TV. That combo of family, food, and a personal video is going to be way more meaningful than anything you could buy.

ok yes I love this plan. dinner at my brother's place because his apartment is bigger than mine and then the video after cake. I'll make her favorite — she loves tres leches cake and I have my abuela's recipe. that plus the video plus everyone together... she's going to lose it I'm already getting emotional thinking about it lol

Homemade tres leches from your abuela's recipe?? That alone would probably make her cry, and then the video on top of it — she's going to have the best birthday. So your to-do list is: coordinate the dinner with Carlos, send out the video request texts this week with a deadline, visit your abuela to record her clip, make the tres leches, and edit the video. Start with the text messages today since you need to give people time to record.

ok sending the group text RIGHT NOW before I forget. I'm putting "NO VERTICAL VIDEOS" in all caps. oh wait should I give people like a prompt or just let them say whatever?? because I know some of my tias are going to freeze up and not know what to say

Great thinking — definitely give them prompts. In the group text, include something like: "Record a 30-60 second video for Mom's birthday! You can share a favorite memory with her, say what you love about her, or just wish her a happy birthday. Keep it simple and from the heart!" That gives people enough direction without making it feel scripted. For the ones who are really camera-shy, tell them it's okay to just say "Happy birthday, we love you" — even a short clip adds to the montage. And if your abuela gets emotional halfway through her clip, keep recording. Those are the best moments.

ay dios mio my abuela is DEFINITELY going to cry on camera and honestly that's going to make the whole video. ok I have the full plan now — group text with prompts, visit abuela to record hers, dinner at Carlos's, tres leches cake, then the video. thank you for helping me plan this I went from zero ideas to a full plan in like 10 minutes. my mom deserves it more than anyone honestly

Your abuela crying on camera will be the moment that breaks everyone. Your mom is going to have an incredible birthday — food she loves, family all together, and a video full of people telling her how much she means to them. That's the kind of gift money can't buy. Send those texts, lock down the date with Carlos, and make extra tres leches because that stuff disappears fast. Have fun with it!
